marketing.pages.about.meta.title: About — The Studio
marketing.pages.about.meta.description: ATLVS Technologies builds the platform for production. ATLVS, GVTEWAY, COMPVSS — three apps, one schema. Shipped by operators who've run the room.
marketing.pages.about.meta.ogImageTitle: Built by the People Who Run the Show.
marketing.pages.about.hero.title: Built by the People Who Run the Show.
marketing.pages.about.hero.lead: ATLVS Technologies builds the platform for production. Every module came out of opening a spreadsheet for the third time and saying "why isn't this in the platform."
marketing.pages.about.hero.subLead: Three apps. One schema. Forty-seven modules. We ship to real productions the weekend before each release. The work decides what stays in.
marketing.pages.about.principles.heading: How We Build.
marketing.pages.about.principles.threeAp: ATLVS, GVTEWAY, COMPVSS share identity, data, and design. One database underneath.
marketing.pages.about.principles.rls.bod: Tenant walled in Postgres, not in the UI. Immutable audit log on every change. Signed webhooks. Standard on every tier.
We ship to a real production the weekend before each release. If it doesn't survive load-in, it doesn't ship.
No per-seat trap. No per-scan tax. No velvet-rope gates on things that should be standard. Pricing reads like one number, not a tax form.
marketing.pages.about.milestones.heading: What Shipped, When.
marketing.pages.about.milestones.procore: RFIs, submittals, daily logs, punch list, payment apps, change orders. Plus everything Procore won't touch.
marketing.pages.about.milestones.threeAp: ATLVS, GVTEWAY, COMPVSS shipped on one database — workspace, portal, field.
marketing.pages.about.milestones.kbyg.bo: One Know-Before-You-Go, authored once, rendered per-persona across portal and field.
marketing.pages.about.milestones.proposa: Twenty-three block types. Live pricing. E-sign in the page. One URL replaces the PDF email thread.
marketing.pages.about.milestones.stripeC: Vendor payouts via Connect. Sixteen typed deliverables with history, comments, attachments.
marketing.pages.about.milestones.firstPr: Staffed and scanned a 2,000-cap show end-to-end on the platform. Stage went down. The platform didn't.
marketing.pages.about.studio.heading: Who Builds It.
marketing.pages.about.studio.body: ATLVS is a production + technology studio. We've staffed residencies, touring runs, private launches, gallery programs, brand activations. We shipped software for every one of them. The platform is a decade of in-the-field instinct, compressed into one workspace.
marketing.pages.about.cta.title: ATLVS Is Open.
marketing.pages.about.cta.subtitle: Free forever for small teams. Per-org pricing the rest of the way up.
marketing.pages.ai-slug.cta.title: ATLVS Is Open.
marketing.pages.ai-slug.cta.subtitle: Free for small teams. AI included on Crew and up.
marketing.pages.ai.meta.title: AI For Production — Drafts From Your Workspace, Never The Public Internet
marketing.pages.ai.meta.description: An assistant that drafts riders, incident reports, call sheets, proposals, recaps, and safety briefs from your live workspace data. Anthropic Claude under the hood; per-org scope; never trained on your data.
marketing.pages.ai.jsonLd.description: Production-AI assistant grounded in your workspace, powered by Anthropic Claude.
marketing.pages.ai.hero.subtitle: An assistant that reads your projects, talent, vendors, schedule, and budgets — then drafts the things production teams write every day. Riders, incident reports, call sheets, proposals, recaps, safety briefs. Anthropic Claude under the hood, scoped to your tenant, never trained on your data.
marketing.pages.ai.drafts.title: What It Drafts.
marketing.pages.ai.drafts.subtitle: Six production workflows where the assistant takes a draft 80% of the way and you ship the last 20%.
marketing.pages.ai.rules.title: What The AI Does — And Won't Do.
marketing.pages.ai.rules.subtitle: Production teams asked us where the lines are. Here's the canon.
marketing.pages.ai.rules.items.0: Reads only your org's data. RLS at the database — not the app.
marketing.pages.ai.rules.items.1: Drafts; does not auto-publish. PM, EHS, or TM holds the publish key.
marketing.pages.ai.rules.items.2: Flags inferences vs. grounded facts. You see what's invented.
marketing.pages.ai.rules.items.3: Conversations log in your tenant, queryable, exportable, not shared.
marketing.pages.ai.rules.items.4: Anthropic Claude — never trained on your records.
marketing.pages.ai.rules.items.5: Per-org cost cap. Runaway usage is impossible.
marketing.pages.ai.faq.title: AI · FAQ
marketing.pages.ai.faq.otherOrgs.q: Does the AI see other organizations' data?
marketing.pages.ai.faq.otherOrgs.a: No. Every tool the assistant can call is scoped to your organization. RLS at the database — your tenant is walled off in Postgres, not in the app layer. The AI cannot reach into anyone else's data, by design.
marketing.pages.ai.faq.training.q: Do you train on our data?
marketing.pages.ai.faq.training.a: No. We don't train models. We're a production platform that uses Anthropic Claude under the hood — your conversations stay in your tenant, your records stay yours.
marketing.pages.ai.faq.model.a: Claude Sonnet 4.6 by default for day-to-day work, Claude Opus 4.7 available per conversation when you need deeper reasoning. Switch in the conversation menu; cost is metered per-org with configurable monthly caps.
marketing.pages.ai.faq.wrong.q: What if the AI gets something wrong?
marketing.pages.ai.faq.wrong.a: Every output is a draft. The PM, EHS lead, or tour manager publishes. Auto-publish workflows are configurable but opt-in. The assistant also flags when it's inferring vs. when it's working from explicit data.
marketing.pages.ai.faq.freeTier.a: Yes. The Free tier includes light AI assistance. Crew and Production tiers include the streaming assistant with conversation history. Festival tier adds custom system prompts and dedicated model selection.
marketing.pages.ai.cta.title: ATLVS Is Open.
marketing.pages.ai.cta.subtitle: Free for small teams. AI included on Crew and up.

marketing.pages.brand-kit.tiles.appDashb: Switchable shell across all three instruments.
marketing.pages.brand-kit.tiles.bridgeKi: Buttons, forms, tables, modals, command palette, auth, 404.
Charts, mobile, notifications, detail, chat, prose, signature.

marketing.pages.brand-kit.tiles.mobileUi: Phone screens — board, detail, wallet, sheet, auth.
marketing.pages.brand-kit.tiles.atlvsPro: Neutral light/dark SaaS theme — dashboard, screens, per-product accent. Distinct from the cosmic brand.
marketing.pages.brand-kit.tiles.proposal: Contract-grade: manifest, schedule, manning, systems, investment.

marketing.pages.careers.metadata.title: Careers — Build for Production
marketing.pages.careers.metadata.descrip: We're a small studio shipping the operating system for live events. Open roles ship work that surfaces on real shows — measured in load-ins survived, not story points burned.
marketing.pages.careers.hero.title: Build for Production.
marketing.pages.careers.hero.body: We're building the platform the live-events industry has needed for fifteen years — one operating system that runs the producer's desk, the crew's phone, and the client's portal off the same database. Small studio, deep ownership, real shows.
marketing.pages.careers.values.heading: How We Crew
marketing.pages.careers.values.crew.body: Small group, deep ownership. Every role touches every shell — marketing, ATLVS, portal, mobile.
marketing.pages.careers.values.tested.bo: We ship to real shows the weekend before each release. Bias to load-in survival over feature theater.
marketing.pages.careers.values.pricing.b: Per org, not per seat. We hold that line internally — no token economies, no perf review caste system.
marketing.pages.careers.values.brandFirs: The platform reads like a magazine. Brutal where it should be, breathing where it can. Code follows.
marketing.pages.careers.values.distribut: Studio in Miami, crew across timezones. Async-first. Travel for site visits and shows, not standups.
marketing.pages.careers.values.equity.bo: Real ownership stake, four-year vest, ten-year exercise window. We pay competitively in cash too.
marketing.pages.careers.roles.heading: Open Roles
marketing.pages.careers.roles.empty.body: We hire deliberately and post openings here when they exist. If your background is unusual or specifically aligned with what we're building, we read every thoughtful intro that comes through the contact form.
marketing.pages.careers.faq.heading: Careers FAQ
marketing.pages.careers.faq.hiring.q: Is the studio hiring right now?
marketing.pages.careers.faq.hiring.a: We're a small team and we hire deliberately. When we do open a role, it lands here first. If nothing's posted, it's truly closed — but we always read thoughtful intros at the contact link below.
marketing.pages.careers.faq.visas.a: For senior engineering and design roles, yes — case by case. We'll be explicit on each posting whether sponsorship is on the table.
marketing.pages.careers.faq.remote.a: Distributed by default. The Miami studio is open if you're nearby and want desk time, but no role requires you to be there.
marketing.pages.careers.faq.interview.a: Three calls: intro with a founder, working session with a peer (no whiteboard puzzles — we look at real work), and a paid trial week if both sides want to keep going.
marketing.pages.careers.faq.notHire.q: What do you NOT hire for?
marketing.pages.careers.faq.notHire.a: Project managers, scrum coaches, and "head of" titles where the work is meetings. Engineering managers code; design leads design; founders sell.
marketing.pages.careers.cta.title: Not Listed? Tell Us Anyway.
marketing.pages.careers.cta.subtitle: If your background is unusual or specifically built for what we ship, write us. The studio reads every note.

marketing.pages.contact.faqs.heading: Contact FAQ
marketing.pages.contact.faqs.start.q: Do I need to call to get started?
marketing.pages.contact.faqs.start.a: No. /signup and you're on Free — forever. 14-day trial on Crew or Production, no card. The studio is for Festival access, custom integrations, or a walkthrough.
marketing.pages.contact.faqs.responseTim: How fast does the studio respond?
Studio: one business day. Concierge on Crew: 24 business hours. Production: 4. Festival: contractual SLA, usually one hour for P0.
marketing.pages.contact.faqs.producer.q: Can we talk to a producer, not a salesperson?
marketing.pages.contact.faqs.producer.a: We'll schedule a call with someone from the studio who's run productions on the platform. Check the box in the form.
marketing.pages.contact.faqs.location.a: Distributed across the US — producers in NYC, LA, Miami, Chicago, Austin. Happy to show up in-person wherever the work is.

marketing.pages.demo.faqs.title: FAQ
marketing.pages.demo.faqs.walkthrough.a: Forty minutes. We open the platform wired to a real production — the canonical one is MMW26 Hialeah. You ask what your show needs, we show you the wiring that runs it. No slide deck.
marketing.pages.demo.faqs.salesCall.a: No. It's a walkthrough. Sales conversations happen if you want them to — most teams open the free tier and start in the platform first.
marketing.pages.demo.faqs.tryWithoutCall: Can I just try it without a call?
Yes. Sign up free — 30 seconds, no card. The walkthrough is for teams that want to see specific workflows wired to their use case.

marketing.pages.help.metadata.title: Help — Crew Support
marketing.pages.help.metadata.descriptio: Self-serve guides, status, and direct support channels. Concierge for customers — triaged by tier. Festival gets an SLA.
marketing.pages.help.hero.title: Crew Support.
marketing.pages.help.hero.subtitle: Self-serve guides for the patient, concierge for the urgent, hotline for the load-in. Choose your channel by how live the show is.
marketing.pages.help.channels.fieldGuide: Long-form guides on every shell — how to run a load-in week, set up advancing, wire Stripe payouts, configure portal access.
What shipped this week. Every release, with context on why it matters at load-in.
For customers — triaged by tier (Free, Crew, Production, Festival). Festival gets a four-hour SLA.
marketing.pages.help.channels.hotline.bo: Live show in trouble? The hotline is staffed by producers, not bots. Available 24h during your event window if you've notified us.
marketing.pages.help.troubleshooting.sig: Try the magic-link flow first — it bypasses password issues. If your email isn't recognized, an admin needs to invite you. The producer or workspace owner can issue an invite from Settings → People.
marketing.pages.help.troubleshooting.web: Check the audit log first (Settings → Audit). Webhooks ship signed; if signature verification fails on your end, the receipt won't register. Re-check the secret + the SHA-256 verification snippet in the docs.
marketing.pages.help.troubleshooting.mob: Pull-to-refresh on the call sheet to force a sync. If a queued action shows as pending for more than 30 seconds, sign out and back in — that re-pairs the offline cache to the active workspace.
marketing.pages.help.faqSection.title: Help FAQ
marketing.pages.help.faqs.reportBug.q: Where do I report a bug?
marketing.pages.help.faqs.reportBug.a: Email support@atlvs.pro with the URL where it happened and a screenshot if you have one. We tag every report with the audit log entry so we can see exactly what happened on our side.
marketing.pages.help.faqs.statusPage.q: Do you have a status page?
marketing.pages.help.faqs.statusPage.a: Yes — status.atlvs.pro. Production incidents post within ten minutes. Maintenance windows announce 48 hours ahead.
marketing.pages.help.faqs.phoneSupport.a: The production hotline is the closest thing — staffed by producers during active event windows. We don't run general phone support; concierge email gets to a human within hours, faster on Festival.
marketing.pages.help.faqs.invoice.q: Where do I find my invoice or change billing details?
marketing.pages.help.faqs.invoice.a: Inside ATLVS: Settings → Billing. Stripe-hosted self-serve, so you can update card, download past invoices, or change billing email there directly.
marketing.pages.help.faqs.cancel.a: Settings → Billing → Cancel subscription. Your data stays available read-only for 90 days; export anything you need from Settings → Exports during that window.
marketing.pages.help.faqs.lostAccess.q: I lost access to my admin account — what now?
marketing.pages.help.faqs.lostAccess.a: Contact concierge from the email on file with the org. We require a chain of identity proof to transfer ownership — typically founder/officer documentation. Plan ~3 business days for the handover.
marketing.pages.help.cta.title: Still Stuck? Talk to a Producer.
marketing.pages.help.cta.subtitle: Concierge email is the fastest path. We respond within hours, faster on Festival.
marketing.pages.integrations.meta.title: Integrations — Stripe, Anthropic, Supabase, Twilio, Resend, Calendar, OAuth, Webhooks
marketing.pages.integrations.meta.descri: The integrations wired in code today — payments, AI, infrastructure, comms, auth, calendar, geo, observability. No coming-soon list; every entry is in production.
marketing.pages.integrations.meta.ogImag: Wired In Code, Not On A Slide.

marketing.pages.integrations.hero.body: The integrations below ship live in production. No coming-soon list, no aspirational entries. Each page links to the technical anchor in the codebase so you can audit before you switch.
marketing.pages.integrations.partner.bod: Open API surface (REST + GraphQL). Verified Partner + Certified tiers. No revenue share, no gatekeeping.
marketing.pages.integrations.cta.title: ATLVS Is Open.
